metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_3646
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Stereotyped developmental elimination of synapses in the Caenorhabditis elegans
hermaphrodite-specific motor neuron (HSNL) is mediated by an E3 ubiquitin ligase, a Skp1-cullin-F-box (SCF) complex composed of SKR-1 (SKP1 homolog) and the F-box protein SEL-10. Figure 3:more skr-1 RNAi-treated animals than control animals had SNB-1::egfp labeled synapses in the secondary synapse region (SSR, (Fig. 3, B and C) suggesting that SKR1 is necessary for proper synapse elimination. Figure 4E, over expression of SKR-1 results in reduced synapse numbers in primary synapse region and SSR showing that SKR-1 is rate-limiting for synapse elimination.. Experimental description: Abstract: Caenorhabditis elegans hermaphrodite-specificmotor neuron (HSNL)
